title: Making Waves for Water Day
story: Every drop counts on World Water Day, celebrated each March 22! People around the globe organize beach cleanups, share water-saving tips on social media, and learn about communities that need clean drinking water. Schools host special assemblies where kids discover how much water they use daily. Many families challenge themselves to shorter showers or fixing leaky faucets. It's a day to splash into action and protect our planet's most precious resource!
activity_question: What do many families challenge themselves to do on World Water Day?
activity_choices: Take shorter showers or fix leaky faucets
Drink only bottled water all day
activity_answer: Take shorter showers or fix leaky faucets
follow_up_question: What's one way your family could save water at home?

title: National Goof Off Day: Permission to Be Silly!
story: Every March 22, people across America get official permission to be completely silly on National Goof Off Day! This wonderfully weird holiday encourages everyone to take a break from serious stuff and just have fun. Kids might spend the day making funny faces in mirrors, telling terrible jokes, or having sock puppet conversations. Adults join in too, wearing mismatched socks to work or eating dessert before dinner. Some families have backwards day celebrations, walking backwards or wearing clothes inside-out. Social media fills up with goofy selfies and silly videos. The whole point is to remember that being playful and laughing is just as important as working hard. It's the one day when acting ridiculous is not just okay, it's encouraged!
activity_question: What do some families do to celebrate National Goof Off Day?
follow_up_question: What's the silliest thing you would do if you had permission to goof off all day?

title: Feel Young Forever Day!
story: Every March 22, people celebrate As Young As You Feel Day by acting however old they want to be! Some grown-ups spend the day playing video games, jumping on trampolines, or eating ice cream for breakfast. Others might dress up in their favorite childhood costumes or have a dance party in their living room. The idea is simple: age is just a number, and you can feel young no matter how many birthdays you've had. Kids love this holiday because adults finally understand that being playful and silly is awesome at any age. It's the perfect excuse to be goofy together!
activity_question: What do some adults do on As Young As You Feel Day?
activity_choices: Play video games and jump on trampolines
activity_answer: Play video games and jump on trampolines
follow_up_question: What would you do if you could feel any age for a whole day?

title: Puerto Rico's Freedom Day Celebration
story: Every March 22, Puerto Rico celebrates Emancipation Day to remember when slavery was abolished on the island. People gather for parades, cultural festivals, and special ceremonies that honor their ancestors' freedom. Schools teach students about this important history through songs, dances, and storytelling. Many families visit museums or attend community events that celebrate Puerto Rican heritage and the courage of those who fought for liberty.
activity_question: How do people in Puerto Rico typically celebrate Emancipation Day?
activity_choices: By eating only ice cream all day
By staying inside and reading quietly
follow_up_question: What other ways could communities celebrate important moments in their history?
